I consider myself having a pretty big hand for a woman (around 12in from thumb to my pinky when stretched), I still feel this mouse is a tad big for me that it wasn't intuitive when grabbing onto it. I also didn't like the edges/ dents for your thumb and ring finger. I now know a slimmer mouse without edges works better for me and I can't recommend this mouse unless you are looking for something less than ten.

description: This wireless mouse features 1000 dpi optical technology for responsive cursor control and 2.4GHz wireless technology for a reliable connection.

reviews.title: Nice design
reviews.text: I use this with my netbook to control it across the room. It is smaller than most other mouse. It goes easily in my travel bag. Nice design. I got tired of plain black.

reviews.title: One of the best everyday mice I've used
reviews.text:
I have input devices for drawing on my tablet, and I have a gaming mouse, but the Logitech M305 is an excellent mouse for every day use, so it's the one I use the most.

The mouse itself has very basic features,
* scroll wheel (mild clicking feedback)with side-click options
* battery status light (green when fully charged, red when almost out)
* two buttons
* an off switch
* a nano USB transmitter than stores inside the mouse
* uses a single AA battery

It does come in jazzy designs, and I liked the yellow/gold one the best. The one thing I might have wanted to change on this mouse is the on/off switch. The best nano-USB mouse I've ever had would automatically turn off when you stored the transmitter inside. With this one, be sure to flip the switch to off as well before you put it in your bag, or it will be down there draining the battery as it moves around.

title: ROCCAT Kone XP Air – Wireless Customizable Ergonomic RGB Gaming Mouse, 19K DPI Optical Sensor, 100-hour Battery & Charging Dock, 29 Programmable Inputs & AIMO RGB Lighting, 4D Wheel – Black
description: The ROCCAT Kone XP Air Wireless Ergonomic RGB Gaming Mouse is designed to put the functionality of actions and macros at your fingertips. Perfected through years of refinement, this mouse is equipped with 10 dedicated buttons plus left, right, and vertical scroll wheel click functionality to expand into 29 programmable inputs, thanks to the Easy-Shift[+] duplicator button. Want to take this mouse on the go? Switch between 2.4GHz Stellar Wireless and Bluetooth for the ultimate in versatility. Additionally, the Kone XP Air’s Charging Dock blends beautifully into your setup and comes equipped with a USB passthrough on the front for the 2.4GHz wireless adapter. To further the immersive RGB experience, the translucent shell and Krystal 4D Wheel set the stage for the Kone XP AIR’s 5 lighting zones, creating stunning lighting effects. ROCCAT AIMO lighting technology produces vibrant RGB lighting displays right out of the box or syncs with other compatible AIMO products. The Kone XP Air packs a performance punch with ROCCAT’s Optical Titan Switches for lightspeed actuation and Owl-Eye 19K dpi optical sensor for precise movements. The heat-treated glides and a Phantom Flex charging cable to even further amplify your unrestricted gameplay, giving you the fastest, smoothest, and most precise experience possible. ERGONOMIC, MULTI-BUTTON DESIGN - Perfected through years of refinement, the beloved Kone ergonomic shape ensures comfort over long gaming sessions. Ten buttons plus 4D wheel expand into 29 programmable inputs thanks to the Easy-Shift[+] duplicator button. STELLAR WIRELESS + BLUETOOTH - Switch between gaming grade 2.4GHz STELLAR WIRELESS and Bluetooth for versatile use. Game all night with up to 100 hours of battery on a full charge. RAPID CHARGE DOCK - The stylish Rapid Charge Dock provides five hours of play time with just 10 minutes of charging. With 4 RGB lighting zones, the dock beautifully accents any desktop setup. Additionally, the 2.4GHz dongle can be plugged into the USB passthrough on the front. AIMO ILLUMINATION - The translucent shell and wheel set the stage for the Kone XP AIR’s five RGB zones, creating a stunning lighting experience. ROCCAT’s AIMO lighting technology produces vibrant RGB lighting displays right out of the box and can sync with other compatible AIMO-enabled products for an immersive lighting display. OWL-EYE OPTICAL SENSOR - Experience smooth, precise tracking thanks to ROCCAT’s Owl-Eye 19K DPI optical sensor. TITAN OPTICAL SWITCHES - ROCCAT’s Titan Optical Switches provide unprecedented speed, and incredible durability with a 100 million click life cycle offering a competitive edge game after game. PHANTOMFLEX CHARGING CABLE - Flexible and light, ROCCAT’s PhantomFlex USB-C charging cable virtually disappears during use to the point that you won't even know it's there. PTFE GLIDES - The Kone XP Air’s heat-treat

reviews.title: Super fun RGB lighting and great for Hell Let Loose
reviews.text: I wanted something with more buttons for Hell Let Loose than the Basilik V3. This mouse is ideal for those that need extra buttons for gaming as that is the primary reason you should consider it. The thumbrest is great as I think all gaming mice should have thumbrests since it’s so much better for your hand ergonomics. I have large hands and the mouse feels very good to hold and gives no fatigue. At first I though my thumb would hit the easy shift button but it didn’t so that’s another plus. I would say the left and right click buttons could be a bit wider and more ergonomic to say something like Basilik v3 which is where that mouse is a bit better but overall negligible. Of course the RGB is where the mouse really looks great, I love seeing so many fun colors move around and it looks really pretty to look at. So if you have a game you really like to play and are annoyed using your keyboard for some critical commands like in Hell Let Loose (map, team chat, squad chat, leadership chat) this is a very pretty, well designed convenient button placement mouse, I would recommend it.

reviews.title: Very nice gaming mouse
reviews.text:
This mouse is very ergonomic, has many buttons, and the software is good. I play many different games and need button profiles for them. The software is easy to use, and it is easy to associate a game/program with a profile for auto-switching when it is run.

I seem to use the buttons/profiles of gaming mice much more than some people. When I read mouse reviews, they mostly talk about the weight of it and other silly things that only first person shooter people care about. I care much more about the number/placement of extra buttons and the software used to configure the mouse.

I have several nice mice from other brands, brought down by HORRIBLE software. Logitech and Corsair make nice mice, but their software is a deal breaker. Roccat's mouse software is WONDERFUL.

I also like that the mouse has optical switches, which will last longer.

Only downsides: Not AS MANY buttons as my Roccat LEADR (started registering the dreaded double-click fault) and the software only supports 5 profiles at once. If you play more than 5 games that need a profile, you have to do the export/import dance. But this is a trivial fault.

The LEDs are a little loud, but I'm not a big LED/RGB lighting person anyway, so you can't fault the mouse. And you can turn them off (though I tend to set the colors to identify the game, so I can make sure I'm in the correct profile.)

Wonderful gaming mice that can be used for many different genres: MMO, FPS, etc.

reviews.title: upgrade from the KONE AIMO
reviews.text: I had to upgrade my previous mouse the KONE AIMO. It has a known issue with the left click which was repaired for a period of time then started acting up. I didn't lose hope in the brand however, because I absolutely love everything else about the AIMO! I play World of Warcraft and use sooo many key binds, so having a mouse like this is very helpful to my gameplay. The Kone XP is awesome. Lighter than the AIMO, similar button style but just feels smoother. Highly recommend it.

reviews.title: Dazzling and customizable...

reviews.text: Leadsail is my go to brand for quality computer mice.  This is the fifth one I have gotten and they all work flawlessly.  This one is slightly more compact in design but their trademark always is an ergonomically satisfying fit in the hand.  I am left handed but use my right hand for the mouse.  I have rather large hands and this mouse is perfect to grip and maneuver.  It also is indeed silent which is not really an issue for me.  What I look for is design, function and durability and you get that with all Leadsail products.  Recommend.

reviews.text: Serves the purpose. Neoprene is very thin. Stretches out of shape easily. Okay if you avoid taking things in and out.

reviews.title: Gets the job done
reviews.text: The adhesion is great! The pouch is very stretchy. Too stretchy for me. My hard drive will slip out of tipped toward the opening. Not worth trying to take it off, since I'm just careful to not tip it opening side down. But I wish it was a little more snug.

reviews.title: Awesome

reviews.title: Good find!
reviews.text: I got this to keep in my work bag as a backup/when I work from home. I will say the first impression wasn't stellar. The top plastic has a cheap look/feel to it. But it also makes up in some nice touches. For example, the USB fob. I was apprehensive about the keep slot for it because there wasn't something that made it feel or look actually secured. But turns out, there's magnetic component so it gets sucked in rather than loosely placed. Battery power seems as advertised. I haven't charged mine in about a month and it's still going. Ergonomics is about what you'd expect based on the photos.

reviews.title: No more Batteries
reviews.text: Love my new mouse! Old one I would turn off and on all the time to save battery. This one I keep wanting to then remember it goes to sleep and no more batteries. First charge went fast, haven't had to charge it since. Love the self storing dongle. Should have purchased this long time ago.

title: Uiosmuph LED Wireless Mouse, G12 Slim Rechargeable Wireless Silent Mouse, 2.4G Portable USB Optical Wireless Computer Mice with USB Receiver and Type C Adapter (Purple)
features: Rechargeable: Built-in rechargeable battery, no need change battery. Special soundless design for the right and left buttons, won’t disturb others.
Colorful Light: 7 different color changes randomly while in use, offering soothing lighting, creating cool and fancy atmosphere for work and play.
Energy Saving: The mouse will be in sleep mode in 8 mins of inactivity. And the LED light can be turned off by the bottom light switch.
Slim & Portable: About 4.4 x 2.2 x 0.79 inches, Perfect for travelling carry and it can be easily put into your laptop bag and ultra-space saving.
Widely Compatibility: Works with Windows 2000/XP/7/8/10/Vista, Mac with USB port or Type C port. Suitable for homes, offices, travel and more.

reviews.title: Great battery life
reviews.text: I'm still not fully decided about how I feel about this mouse. But, I can talk about what I know so far. The packaging I got the mouse and was really nice, really simple, but easy to open. The mouse was easy to set up, and the RGB looks pretty cool. There is a really cool DPI changer function that makes gaming much easier, however, I can't recommend this mouse for gaming because the ergonomics are pretty trash. I have to cramp my hand like a claw over the top of the mouse and after long periods of use it does start hurting. The surface is also pretty slippery, so I found that my hand slips on the mouse all the time. The tracking however, is great! And I would have to say that biggest plus is the battery life. I got this about 2 weeks ago, charged it once, and it is yet to die! I think the features of this mouse are great, but I would not recommend it. Purely because of the ergonomics, if you are going to use this mouse often, then you probably should go if something more ergonomic.

reviews.title: Poor performance | no charge indicator
reviews.text: Highly susceptible to interference. Drags and lags.

reviews.title: Meh
reviews.text: I like the size and the LED colors.  The battery life is a little lackluster and it actually starts acting up when it's "tired".  I got it for my iPad but haven't used it a month or so b/c I got tired of her attitude.

reviews.title: Had 2 of these and they both died
reviews.text: Will never buy from this company again... the second one we bought froze and would not work again.. same as the first one.

reviews.title: Basic but functions well
reviews.text: I ordered a ‘LED Wireless Mouse, Uiosmuph G12 Slim Rechargeable Wireless Silent Mouse, 2.4G Portable USB Optical Wireless Computer Mice with USB Receiver and Type C Adapter (Silver)’ for review.  For the price point, this is a basic mouse.  The rotating light strip is a cool looking feature.  It is sleek in design.  It connected well with the dongle to my Surface Pro.  It glides well over a mouse pad.  It is rechargeable, which I prefer to battery operated.  The click of this mouse is quiet. The dongle slides into the bottom and of course it has already been lost.  I prefer the dongle to be stored in a closed compartment on a mouse.  It does not require recharging for several days of moderate use and seems to charge quickly.  Overall, for basic use, this mouse works well.

reviews.title: Nice looking but.....
reviews.text:
This mouse looks like the picture. the USB neatly stores into the bottom of the car.
What I didn't like about it is:
1. One of the wheels has rough edges and where the battery fits is also a rough edge. They catch on my mouse pad, a lot.
Only one of the wheels move. If they all did, I think it would make the mouse smoother when you are working with it.
I didn't use it in time to return it so I am stuck with it. I think I may have to try and file it down.

reviews.title: Cheap and useless.
reviews.text: This mouse feels so cheap, it wasn’t even worth giving a review but I wanted to warn others to not get this mouse. It’s so flimsy and made so cheaply, I didn’t waste anymore time with it so I am returning.

reviews.title: Flawless Bluetooth connectivity with Mac
reviews.text: My Logitech M310 (been using them for 10-15 years) could not connect properly with my USB-C/Thunderbolt MacBookPro, using their USB dongle, thru a USB adapter. Scroll wheel would NOT work, and Logitech tech support said they would not update drivers. 'Don't use an adapter.' But they aren't aware that new Macs don't have old-school USB ports? Anyone else have this problem?! Anyway... this Seenda connects directly with Bluetooth, and connects instantly. If it sleeps (after a few hours?) it wakes and re-connects with one click, in less than a second. I charged the battery when I bought it a few weeks ago, and forgot that I might need to charge it again one day. Still going. Downsides: it is much smaller and lighter than I am used to (the M310 is easily twice as thick/tall) so I am getting used to that. Took awhile to find the right DPI/resolution/speed (and the button to change that is in a precarious, easy-to-hit-by-mistake position. Overall a great recommend.

reviews.title: squeaks when scrolling - not for small hands
reviews.text: Loud clicks, SQUEAKS when scrolling, made for larger hands. You would think since it's pink it wouldn't be designed for a mans hands but here we are. Ultimately, the obnoxious squeaking while scrolling is why I am returning it.

reviews.title: Works great but... The wheel squeaks!
reviews.text: This mouse works great but the wheel squeaks!

reviews.title: Hey it works great HIGHLY RECOMMEND
reviews.text:
It works does random motion immediately recognized by my dell laptop workstation
I’m not monitored by my boss so can’t give insight to that consideration. IT KEEPS MY SCREEN open so I don’t have to keep logging in
Wish the jiggle button had a light to tell it’s active HOWEVER perhaps this is a good thing (it immediately jiggles so I know it’s working) no light no one knows around me it’s on
I’d highly recommend this

reviews.title: Great Wireless Mouse and Jiggler
reviews.text: This mouse is the perfect size and weight, moves well and clicks quietly. I love the jigglier functionality; you just press the button built into the mouse and it keep the mouse cursor moving on the screen. It keeps my window open and Office teams active. I should have gotten a mouse like this long ago; it allows you additional freedom at work when you need to be away from your desk for a while. Highly recommend!

reviews.title: Very good idea
reviews.text: Very good idea to have this jiggler built in, other similar products need to have an additional equipment, and put mouse on it for moving, not as convenient as this one. But sometimes this product is not stable, after a couple of hours working, I found the mouse stopped moving, I checked and found the battery light was off due to unknown reason, and after I reset the battery, the mouse moved again, and such kinds of issue happened several times. Therefore, I can only gave 4 stars review. But anyway I like this cute tool.

reviews.title: Keep screensavers from activating or keep from being logged off for no activity!
reviews.text:
A very handy, take it with you mouse that will keep pretty much anything from timing out due to lack of activity. When you need to step away for a few minutes and not have anything you are working on, just press the button on top to activate the jiggler and keep the mouse pointer gently moving.
I have only had this for a week or so and it has already proved it's worth. When my screen saver kicks off any process I have running slows way down. When I am rendering media this is a major pain, or I should say "used to be a major pain".
The dongle for USB connectivity stores in the mouse itself so you can carry the mouse and never have to worry about losing the dongle. It also has an on/off switch to save battery life.
A nice piece of gear, I suggest this for anyone that doesn't want to time out again.

reviews.title: Quiet and Functional
reviews.text:
I have been searching for a product like this for a long time! I work remotely most days and have to rely on connecting through a VPN connection. The connection automatically disconnects after a certain time frame, so this jiggler function is great! I can click the button to activate and leave the computer.
The setup on this mouse is so simple. I plugged in the USB and the mouse worked right away.
My favorite feature that was unexpected was how quiet the mouse is. I wasn’t use to not being able to hear a click. Such a nice product!

reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:5a9baeffb5013de01b566ac34f1da8a5]] Love the way this keyboard sounds as I type.  I also like that the round keys allow a little more room in between keys and that I don’t make as many errors while typing.  The color is great and the design is think and sleek.

reviews.title: my favorite keyboard
reviews.text: This keyboard is the best! It is comfortable to use, matches my desk setup perfectly, and best of all, the keys AND the mouse are basically totally silent! My 10-month-old is a VERY light sleeper! Everything wakes her up, but not these. A major win for me! Highly recommend. 100% I would buy this again! (batteries are not included. You need one aa battery for the keyboard and also the mouse)

reviews.title: Wired + Wireless?  Work for you? Good multimedia controls & phone slot
reviews.text:
Not a fan of wireless accessories (the batteries typically fail without warning, makers want you to be surprised), I made an exception for this pairing model because the keyboard is wired.

PRO
-Ubiquitous AA battery for mouse
-Economical
-Numeric pad
-Multimedia buttons: Volume knob, buttons for mute, pause, previous, next
-A place to put your phone (nice touch!)

CON
-Neither is backlit
-KB not programmable

NEUTRAL
-Non-'clack-y' key design, these are "quiet"
-Some humans will like pink, not widely available, maybe a good gift
-Con (for me) is the wireless mouse

reviews.title: Fun cordless mouse and keyboard
reviews.text: This is a great little retro cordless keyboard and mouse combo. I ordered it because it has just one dongle that both operate from. That gives me more USB ports available for other things. It works great as long as the keyboard and mouse are close to the laptop or the tower. It lags a bit when it’s in a keyboard drawer. I like the compact and portable nature this setup includes!

reviews.title: pink keyboard and wireless mouse
reviews.text: Love the pink color of this retro keyboard and mouse.  Great that the keyboard is spill proof I can have my coffee and not worry about wrecking my keyboard.  Easily plugged right into my all-in-one computer.  Took me a little while to get used to the punk keycaps, and now I love how quiet and comfortable they are to use.  The mouse fits well in my hand, and the wheel rolls back and forth smoothly. Top 5 stars for this one!

title: Logitech M585 Multi-Device Wireless Mouse – Control and Move Text/Images/Files Between 2 Windows and Apple Mac Computers and Laptops with Bluetooth or USB, 2 Year Battery Life, Graphite
description: Talk about a game changer! With Logitech FLOW, M585 Multi-Device has the almost magical capacity to navigate seamlessly on two computers and copy-paste from one to the other – even if you're using a PC and a Mac. It certainly beats sending yourself emails. M585 also comes with a host of other features to improve your productivity, your way: fast and easy scrolling through long web pages; five programmable buttons, including two handy thumb buttons; a 2-year battery life** and a curved, domed shape that supports your palm comfortably during long hours of work or play. Dual connectivity (Bluetooth or 2.4 GHz Logitech Unifying). DUAL CONNECTIVITY - Use with up to three Windows or Mac computers and laptops via included Unifying receiver or Bluetooth Smart wireless technology
LONG BATTERY LIFE- The Logitech M585 just keeps going, with 2 years of power on a single AA battery

reviews.title: Excellent travel size and 6 month long battery life!
reviews.text:
Updated 1 year later(May 3, 2020)

Down grading from 5 stars to 3 stars because it turns out there might be a bug in the mouse that once you pair it with 2 different computers over bluetooth, you cannot reset the mouse to use the wireless dongle. There is also no support on the Logitech website - try searching their website for the manual for this mouse, it's absolutely missing. You'll see an FAQ section and some various sections on tech spec, but no setup guide or manual!

*********

If you're tried of replacing batteries, this mouse is for you! I'm impressed by how long a single AA battery lasts in this mouse. I haven't had to replace the battery since getting it 6 months ago even with daily use. The two modes is nice since it lets me connect this mouse to two computers! The bluetooth is strong and connects well to my MacBook Pro, while the included radio frequency receiver is plugged into a iMac and I can switch between computers with a. simple button click.

reviews.title: Great travel mouse!
reviews.text: Love the feel of it. Easy to connect. Small enough to use for travel yet big enough to where it doesn’t hurt to use ergonomically.

reviews.title: WOW! Seamless movement from one computer to the other!
reviews.text:
This device synched to both computers quickly. Download the 'Logi Options' software on both, then enable 'Flow'
Move the mouse off the bottom of one screen & it appears at the top of the other laptop.
Copy a file on computer one, switch to the computer two, right-click->paste. You have copied the file took computer one to computer two.
The mouse itself is legit comfortable, precise asks smooth.
Add 'Flow' and it is also revolutionary.
Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Nice mouse for Mac Pro using Big Sur OS
reviews.text: I was looking for a mouse for my MacPro computer.  It's got 2 buttons on the mouse that I wanted to setup as volume up, and volume down.  But there is no instructions on how to do this, and I can't find any.  I'm using the mouse as a standard mouse with scroll for web pages.  Works perfect for that.

reviews.title: Nice and quiet
reviews.text:
Nice, quiet mouse at a great price. It's on the smaller size, just what I needed for carrying in the computer bag. I have long fingers, but not necessarily large hands, and this fits perfectly for me. The scroll wheel is nice and smooth (and also quiet). You can feel some tactile "knurling" when you scroll, so it's not baby's-butt-smooth, but it's not like scrolling over a circular-saw blade either.

Using the USB dongle, the mouse is very responsive. The Bluetooth connection seemed a little laggy to me. No stars off unless it does the same when I test it on another PC.

I do not intend to install the Logitech Options software so I cannot speak to the usefulness of Flow or reassigning buttons.

Pros
- USB and Bluetooth connectivity
- Can use with up to two devices.
- 7 buttons: L, R, Center, Center-Left, Center-Right, Back, Forward. Eight if you include the PC selector button.
- Very quiet left/right click buttons
- Nearly quiet center click and back/forward buttons
- Uses one AA battery (preferred over non-replaceable battery)
- Battery life advertised at 2 years.
- Has an on/off switch on the bottom

Cons
- Possible laggy response using Bluetooth. I need to test on another PC to be sure.

Other consideration:
- The USB dongle storage is behind the battery door. If you frequently remove the dongle from the PC, this might be a concern for you (it's not a problem for me).
